name: 36 · Audio Transcribe (VAD → data channel → faster-whisper)
description:
Streams real audio bytes between two Python steps over a docs/25 DATA channel, proving the binary data plane carries audio end-to-end.

  Start{audio_b64} → vad (decode WAV, energy-gate VAD, re-emit voiced PCM on Data/Out channel `speech`) → transcribe (stream('speech') → faster-whisper tiny) → End{transcript}.

The audio arrives base64-encoded inline in the Start token (field `audio_b64`) so the demo runs off plain JSON — no file upload / object store in the loop. The `vad` step decodes the WAV, runs a pure-Python `audioop` RMS energy gate over 30 ms frames, and writes ONLY the voiced frames as raw 16-bit PCM (~100 ms per write) over the `speech` data channel. The bulk audio rides the out-of-band JetStream datastream transport — it NEVER enters the net marking; the net sees only the channel's open + close (2 firings for the whole stream). The `transcribe` step drains the byte stream via `stream('speech')` (starting EARLY, while the VAD step is still producing), reassembles the PCM, and runs faster-whisper (`tiny`, CPU/int8) to produce the transcript.

Audio is a friendly payload for the JetStream data channel: a 30 ms 16 kHz frame is ~960 bytes at ~50/s — far under the message cap, so no S3 adapter is needed (S3 is for multi-GB elements like 4K frames). The data plane is durable + reliable-ordered, which is exactly right for transcription (no dropped speech).

LIVE-ONLY, run via the API (not `mekhan test`): the `transcribe` step first pip-installs faster-whisper + ctranslate2/numpy and downloads the `tiny` model (~75 MB) into the venv/HF cache, which exceeds the 60 s test cap on a cold run; warm runs are fast. A self-contained sample is bundled at `assets/sample.wav` (generated via macOS `say`, says 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Streaming audio over a data channel works.'); base64-encode it into `start_tokens[0].token.audio_b64` and POST to `/api/v1/instances`. The compile shape (binary data channel) is covered offline by demo 14; this demo's value is the live audio round-trip + transcription.
